questions.promptText: What is 15% of 240?
A train 150m long crosses a pole in 15 seconds. Find its speed in km/hr.
If x+y=10 and x-y=4, find xy.
questions.explanation: Solving gives x=7, y=3, so xy=21.

questions.promptText: The average of 5 numbers is 20. If one number is excluded, the average becomes 18. Find the excluded number.
questions.explanation: Sum of 5 numbers=100, sum of remaining 4=72, so excluded number=28.

questions.promptText: A shopkeeper marks an item 25% above cost price and gives a 10% discount. Find his profit percentage.
Simple interest on Rs. 5000 at 8% per annum for 3 years is:
Find the compound interest on Rs. 2000 at 10% per annum for 2 years, compounded annually.
The ratio of two numbers is 3:5 and their sum is 96. Find the larger number.
questions.explanation: 3x+5x=96 gives x=12, so the larger number = 5x=60.

questions.promptText: A can complete a work in 12 days and B in 18 days. Working together, how many days will they take?
questions.explanation: Combined rate = 1/12+1/18 = 5/36 per day, so time = 36/5 = 7.2 days.

questions.promptText: Find the value of x if 2x + 3 = 15.
A sum of money doubles itself in 8 years at simple interest. Find the rate of interest.
questions.explanation: Since SI equals the principal, R = 100/8 = 12.5%.

questions.promptText: The perimeter of a rectangle is 60cm and its length is twice its breadth. Find the area.
questions.explanation: l+b=30 and l=2b give b=10, l=20, so area=200 sq cm.

questions.promptText: What is the probability of getting a sum of 7 when two dice are rolled?
questions.explanation: There are 6 favorable outcomes out of 36 total, giving 6/36=1/6.

questions.promptText: If the cost price of 20 articles equals the selling price of 16 articles, find the profit percentage.
questions.explanation: CP of 20 = SP of 16 means SP per article = 1.25x CP, giving 25% profit.

questions.promptText: Find the next term: 5, 11, 19, 29, 41, ?
questions.explanation: Differences increase by 2 each time (6,8,10,12,14), so 41+14=55.

questions.promptText: A boat travels 30 km downstream in 2 hours and returns upstream in 3 hours. Find the speed of the boat in still water.
questions.explanation: Downstream speed=15, upstream speed=10, so still-water speed=(15+10)/2=12.5 km/hr.

questions.promptText: The LCM of 12 and 18 is:
questions.explanation: The least common multiple of 12 and 18 is 36.

questions.promptText: The HCF of 24 and 36 is:
questions.explanation: The highest common factor of 24 and 36 is 12.

questions.promptText: A man's age is 4 times his son's age. After 20 years, his age will be twice his son's age. Find the son's current age.
questions.explanation: Solving 4x+20=2(x+20) gives x=10.

questions.promptText: Find the value: (3^2 x 3^3) / 3^4
questions.explanation: Using exponent rules: 3^(2+3-4)=3^1=3.

questions.promptText: A car covers a distance in 5 hours at 60 km/hr. What speed is required to cover the same distance in 4 hours?
questions.explanation: Distance=300 km, so required speed=300/4=75 km/hr.

questions.promptText: If the price of an item increases by 20%, by what percentage should consumption be reduced to keep expenditure the same?
Find the missing number: 4, 9, 16, 25, 36, ?
questions.explanation: These are perfect squares; the next is 7^2=49.

questions.promptText: Two pipes A and B can fill a tank in 10 hours and 15 hours respectively. If both are opened together, how long will it take to fill the tank?
questions.explanation: Combined rate=1/10+1/15=1/6, so time=6 hours.

questions.promptText: The sum of the first 20 natural numbers is:
A sells an article to B at a profit of 20% and B sells it to C at a profit of 10%. If C pays Rs. 264, find the cost price for A.
If 5 men can complete a job in 12 days, how many days will 6 men take?
questions.explanation: Total work=5x12=60 man-days, so 6 men take 60/6=10 days.

questions.promptText: Find the value of the square root of 225.
questions.explanation: The square root of 225 is 15.

questions.promptText: A number is increased by 25% and then decreased by 20%. Find the net percentage change.
questions.explanation: 1.25 x 0.8 = 1.0, meaning there is no net change.

questions.promptText: The average of the first 10 odd numbers is:
questions.explanation: The sum of the first 10 odd numbers is 100, so the average is 10.

questions.promptText: A train running at 72 km/hr crosses a platform 250m long in 26 seconds. Find the length of the train.
questions.explanation: Speed=20 m/s, distance covered=20x26=520m, so train length=520-250=270m.

questions.promptText: If A:B = 2:3 and B:C = 4:5, find A:B:C.
questions.explanation: Scaling A:B and B:C to a common B value of 12 gives A:B:C = 8:12:15.

questions.promptText: Find the compound interest on Rs. 10000 for 1 year at 10% compounded half-yearly.
questions.explanation: Rate per half-year=5%, amount=10000x1.05^2=11025, so CI=1025.

questions.promptText: The sum of three consecutive even numbers is 90. Find the largest number.
questions.explanation: Let the numbers be x, x+2, x+4; 3x+6=90 gives x=28, so the largest is 32.

questions.promptText: A vessel contains milk and water in ratio 3:1. If 8 liters of water is added, the ratio becomes 3:2. Find the initial quantity of milk.
questions.explanation: Let milk=3x, water=x; solving (x+8)/3x ratio gives x=8, so milk=24 liters.

questions.promptText: What is the compound interest formula for amount A, given Principal P, rate r%, and time n years?
questions.explanation: This is the standard compound interest formula.

questions.promptText:
What is the value of x?
Statement I: x + 5 = 12.
Statement II: 2x = 14.

options.text: Statement I alone is sufficient, but Statement II alone is not
Statement II alone is sufficient, but Statement I alone is not
Both statements together are sufficient, but neither alone is sufficient

questions.explanation: Both statements independently give x=7.

questions.promptText:
Is n divisible by 6?
Statement I: n is divisible by 12.
Statement II: n is divisible by 4.

options.text: Statement I alone is sufficient, but Statement II alone is not
Statement II alone is sufficient, but Statement I alone is not
Both statements together are sufficient, but neither alone is sufficient

questions.explanation: Divisible by 12 guarantees divisible by 6, but divisible by 4 alone does not.

questions.promptText:
What is the area of a rectangle?
Statement I: The length is 10cm.
Statement II: The perimeter is 30cm.

options.text: Statement I alone is sufficient, but Statement II alone is not
Statement II alone is sufficient, but Statement I alone is not
Both statements together are sufficient, but neither alone is sufficient

questions.explanation: Length alone or perimeter alone isn't enough, but together they give breadth=5, area=50.

questions.promptText:
Is x > y?
Statement I: x + 3 > y + 3.
Statement II: x - 2 > y - 2.

options.text: Statement I alone is sufficient, but Statement II alone is not
Statement II alone is sufficient, but Statement I alone is not
Both statements together are sufficient, but neither alone is sufficient

questions.explanation: Both statements simplify directly to x > y independently.

questions.promptText:
What is the value of a two-digit number?
Statement I: The number is a multiple of 9.
Statement II: The number is between 50 and 60.

options.text: Statement I alone is sufficient, but Statement II alone is not
Statement II alone is sufficient, but Statement I alone is not
Both statements together are sufficient, but neither alone is sufficient

questions.explanation: Only when combined do the constraints narrow to a single number, 54.

questions.promptText:
Is triangle ABC a right triangle?
Statement I: One angle is 90 degrees.
Statement II: The sides satisfy a^2 + b^2 = c^2.

options.text: Statement I alone is sufficient, but Statement II alone is not
Statement II alone is sufficient, but Statement I alone is not
Both statements together are sufficient, but neither alone is sufficient

questions.explanation: Each statement independently confirms a right triangle.

questions.promptText:
What is the value of y?
Statement I: y is a prime number less than 5.
Statement II: y is an even number.

options.text: Statement I alone is sufficient, but Statement II alone is not
Statement II alone is sufficient, but Statement I alone is not
Both statements together are sufficient, but neither alone is sufficient

questions.explanation: Combined, the only even prime less than 5 is 2.

questions.promptText:
How many employees are in the department?
Statement I: The department has more than 10 but fewer than 15 employees.
Statement II: The number of employees is a multiple of 4.

options.text: Statement I alone is sufficient, but Statement II alone is not
Statement II alone is sufficient, but Statement I alone is not
Both statements together are sufficient, but neither alone is sufficient

questions.explanation: Combined, the only multiple of 4 between 10 and 15 is 12.

questions.promptText:
Is x positive?
Statement I: x^2 = 16.
Statement II: x^3 = -64.

options.text: Statement I alone is sufficient, but Statement II alone is not
Statement II alone is sufficient, but Statement I alone is not
Both statements together are sufficient, but neither alone is sufficient

questions.explanation: Statement II gives a unique real cube root, x=-4, so x is negative; Statement I alone allows x=4 or -4.

questions.promptText:
The table shows sales (units) of products P, Q, R over four months:
Jan: P=120, Q=90, R=150
Feb: P=140, Q=100, R=130
Mar: P=160, Q=110, R=170
Apr: P=150, Q=120, R=160

What is the total sales of product P over the four months?

The table shows sales (units) of products P, Q, R over four months:
Jan: P=120, Q=90, R=150
Feb: P=140, Q=100, R=130
Mar: P=160, Q=110, R=170
Apr: P=150, Q=120, R=160

In which month was the total sales (P+Q+R) highest?
questions.explanation: Monthly totals: Jan=360, Feb=370, Mar=440, Apr=430; March is highest.

What is the amount allocated to R&D?

A company's annual budget of Rs. 20,00,000 is allocated as: Marketing 25%, R&D 30%, Operations 20%, HR 15%, Admin 10%.

What is the combined amount allocated to HR and Admin?

How much more is allocated to Marketing than to Operations?

A company's annual budget of Rs. 20,00,000 is allocated as: Marketing 25%, R&D 30%, Operations 20%, HR 15%, Admin 10%.

If the R&D allocation is increased by 10 percentage points, what would be the new R&D percentage?

A company's annual budget of Rs. 20,00,000 is allocated as: Marketing 25%, R&D 30%, Operations 20%, HR 15%, Admin 10%.

What fraction of the total budget is allocated to Marketing and R&D combined?

The table shows student enrollment in 4 courses over two years:
Course A: Year1=200, Year2=250
Course B: Year1=150, Year2=180
Course C: Year1=300, Year2=280
Course D: Year1=100, Year2=140

Which course saw a decline in enrollment from Year1 to Year2?
questions.explanation: Course C dropped from 300 to 280.

Course A: Year1=200, Year2=250
Course B: Year1=150, Year2=180
Course C: Year1=300, Year2=280
Course D: Year1=100, Year2=140

Course A: Year1=200, Year2=250
Course B: Year1=150, Year2=180
Course C: Year1=300, Year2=280

questions.explanation: Course C had 300 students in Year1, the highest.

questions.promptText:
The table shows student enrollment in 4 courses over two years:
Course A: Year1=200, Year2=250
Course B: Year1=150, Year2=180
Course C: Year1=300, Year2=280
Course D: Year1=100, Year2=140

What was the overall percentage change in total enrollment from Year1 to Year2?
questions.explanation: Year1 total=750, Year2 total=850, change=(850-750)/750 x 100=13.33%.
